**Acceptance Criteria**

The bracket flow now substantially addresses the prior coverage-run feedback: [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md:378) explicitly runs `jit gate pass <B-UUID> <coverage_gate_preset>`, and [crates/jit/tests/bracket_coverage_gate_run_test.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/bracket_coverage_gate_run_test.rs:221) verifies the standard runner persists `GateRunResult` and updates `B` gate status.

Blocking issue: the skill’s approved-plan check example uses the wrong JSON field, `gate_status`, at [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md:122). The actual output field is `gates_status`. As written, the documented manual bracket flow can fail to display the plan gate status it requires before fan-out.

**Prior Review Feedback**

The latest prior finding about fabricated coverage status is addressed. [crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:429) leaves the coverage gate pending, and the skill now delegates execution to the standard gate runner. The new integration test covers pass and fail persisted gate-run outcomes.

**JIT Issue Management**

The prerequisite issue `17eb97ba` is done. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with label warnings only, which are allowed by the review instructions.

**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

No storage boundary violation found in the bracket helper. It continues to use `IssueStore` APIs. The command-layer choice to keep `bracket_breakdown_with_config` structural is consistent with the documented workflow, where gate execution is handled by the existing gate runner.

**Functional Paradigm**

The command code still uses imperative sequencing for ordered graph mutations, which is appropriate in this orchestration layer. No blocking functional-style issue found.

**Correctness And Invariants**

Gate-run consistency is improved: the helper no longer manually stamps gate status, and the documented workflow uses the real gate runner. Source/sink wiring and cycle prevalidation remain covered in [crates/jit/tests/bracket_breakdown_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/bracket_breakdown_tests.rs:247).

The remaining correctness concern is the wrong `gate_status` field in the skill doc’s approved-plan command, because it undermines the required “consume an approved plan” operator check.

**Safety**

No unsafe code observed in the reviewed paths. PID-safety-sensitive code was not changed by this issue.

**Testing**

Blocking: the workspace test gate does not currently pass in this environment. I ran:

`cargo fmt --all -- --check && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --workspace --quiet`

`fmt` and `clippy` completed, but `cargo test --workspace` failed with 5 failing tests:

- [crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:895) `test_claim_issue_enforces_prechecks`
- [crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:954) `test_claim_issue_succeeds_when_prechecks_pass`
- [crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:710) `test_find_available_port_returns_free_port`
- [crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:719) `test_find_available_port_skips_bound_port`
- [crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:813) `test_start_server_errors_when_child_exits_immediately`

I reran the two claim tests individually; both still failed. The bracket-specific tests passed locally: `cargo test -p jit --test bracket_breakdown_tests --test bracket_coverage_gate_run_test --quiet`.

**Error Handling**

The bracket helper still returns contextual errors for unapproved plans and cyclic child graphs. No new library `unwrap()` / `expect()` issue found in the changed production paths.

**CLI Contract**

No new CLI command surface was added. Existing commands used by the skill support `--json` where needed. The skill doc typo should be fixed to query `gates_status`.

**Documentation**

Blocking documentation defect: [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md:122) references `gate_status` instead of `gates_status`.

VERDICT: FAIL
